name: Martinique (France)

colors.symbolism: the blood shed for redemption and liberty, in the pan-African tradition of Marcus Garvey's flag
the luxuriant vegetation of the African Motherland
the African descent of the Martinican people

description: A field divided per pall (three regions meeting at the centre of the flag): green above, black below, and a red isosceles triangle along the hoist whose apex meets the horizontal meridian at the centre.
history: Known as the rouge-vert-noir, the flag was designed in 1968 by Guy Cabort-Masson and Alex Ferdinand as the flag of the Martinican independence movement, its colours taken from Marcus Garvey's pan-African flag. It reached the final round of the 2022-2023 public consultation on a territorial flag and finished second; after the winning hummingbird design was withdrawn over a plagiarism controversy, the Assembly of Martinique adopted the rouge-vert-noir on 2 February 2023 with 44 votes in favour and one abstention. The French Tricolore retains official standing alongside it, as Martinique remains a French overseas department and region.

features.symbolism: the field is divided per pall into three triangles — green above, black below, and a red triangle along the hoist, in the red-green-black colours of Marcus Garvey's pan-African flag long used as the emblem of the Martinican independence movement
